breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 1 SCOPE
text: 1 SCOPE

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 1 SCOPE > Clause 1.1 This specification shall be followed for the fabrication of all types of welded joints of piping
text:
1.1. This specification shall be followed for the fabrication of all types of welded joints of piping
system with in battery limits of the plant .

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 1 SCOPE
text: The welded pipe joints shall include the following:

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 1 SCOPE
text:
a. All pipe joints, longitudinal butt weld (for Tanks / Plate works), circumferencial butt weld
and socket weld.
b. Attachment of forging, flanges and other supports to pipe.
c. Welded manifold header and other sub assemblies.
d. Welded branch connections with or without RF Pads
e. Joints in welded /fabricated piping components
f. The attachment of smaller connections for vents/drains/drips/and other instrument
tappings.
Any approval granted by Owner/CONSULTANT shall not relieve the Contractor of his
responsibilities and guarantee.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 2 APPLICABLE CODES & STANDARDS
text: 2 APPLICABLE CODES & STANDARDS

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 2 APPLICABLE CODES & STANDARDS
text:
All welding works, equipment for welding, heat treatement, other auxillury function and the
welding personnel shall meet the requirement of latest edition of the following accepted
standards and procedures.
§ IBR Indian Boiler Regulations
§ ASME Section II, Part C Welding Rods, Electrodes and Filler Metals.
§ ASME Section V Non-destructive Examination.
§ ASME Section IX Welding and Brazing Qualifications.
§ ASME B31.1 Power Piping.
§ ASME B31.3 Process Piping
§ ASME Section VIII Boiler and Pressure Vessel code
§ All applicable Licensor’s specifications

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 2 APPLICABLE CODES & STANDARDS
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 2 APPLICABLE CODES & STANDARDS
text:
In the event of any differences due to the additional requirements mentioned in this
specification over and above those obligatory as per code this specification shall be binding.
However in the event of any differences due to the additional requirements mentioned in any
applicable specification (Example:- Statutory, Licensor specification etc.) mentioned in the
tender, the most stringent requirement shall govern.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 3 BASE METAL
text: 3 BASE METAL

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 3 BASE METAL > Clause 3.1 In general, use of carbon steel, alloy steel and stainless steel is envisaged. The details of the
text:
3.1. In general, use of carbon steel, alloy steel and stainless steel is envisaged. The details of the
material specifications are given in the welding specification chart.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 3 BASE METAL > Clause 3.2 The Contractor shall provide manufacturer test certificate for every heat number of the
text:
3.2. The Contractor shall provide manufacturer test certificate for every heat number of the
materials supplied by him.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 4 WELDING CONSUMABLES
text: 4 WELDING CONSUMABLES

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 4 WELDING CONSUMABLES > Clause 4.1 The Contractor shall provide at his own expense all the welding consumables necessary for
text:
4.1. The Contractor shall provide at his own expense all the welding consumables necessary for
the execution of the job such as electrodes, filler wire, argon etc. and this should bear approval
of Owner / CONSULTANT.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 4 WELDING CONSUMABLES > Clause 4.2 The welding electrode and filler wire supplied by the Contractor shall conform to the class
text:
4.2. The welding electrode and filler wire supplied by the Contractor shall conform to the class
specified in the welding specification chart.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 4 WELDING CONSUMABLES > Clause 4.3 The Contractor shall submit batch test certificate from the electrode manufacturers giving
text:
4.3. The Contractor shall submit batch test certificate from the electrode manufacturers giving
details of physical and chemical test for each batch of electrodes to be used.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 4 WELDING CONSUMABLES > Clause 4.4 All low hydrogen type of electrode shall be rebaked at 350 deg C for one hour minimum and
text:
4.4. All low hydrogen type of electrode shall be rebaked at 350 deg C for one hour minimum and
stored in ovens kept at 80 to 100 deg C before use or as per manufactur spec.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 4 WELDING CONSUMABLES > Clause 4.5 The electrodes, filler wires and flux used shall be free from rust, oil, grease, earth and other
text:
4.5. The electrodes, filler wires and flux used shall be free from rust, oil, grease, earth and other
foreign matter affect the quality of welding.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 4 WELDING CONSUMABLES > Clause 4.6 Tungsten electrodes used shall confirm to ASME Sec II C SFA 5.12 specification.
text: 4.6. Tungsten electrodes used shall confirm to ASME Sec II C SFA 5.12 specification.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS
text: 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS > Clause 5.1 Argon gas used in GTAW welding for shielding purpose shall be 99.995% pure. The purity of
text:
5.1. Argon gas used in GTAW welding for shielding purpose shall be 99.995% pure. The purity of
the gas shall be certified by the manufacturer. The rate of flow for shielding purpose shall be
established through procedure qualification test.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS > Clause 5.2 Argon gas with a purity level of 99.995% shall be used for purging.
text: 5.2. Argon gas with a purity level of 99.995% shall be used for purging.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS > Clause 5.3 The purging shall be maintained during root pass and first filling pass to minimize oxidation
text:
5.3. The purging shall be maintained during root pass and first filling pass to minimize oxidation
on the inner side of the pipe.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS > Clause 5.4 Initial purging shall be maintained for the sufficient period of time to remove trapped air. In no
text: 5.4. Initial purging shall be maintained for the sufficient period of time to remove trapped air. In no

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS
text:
case the initial purging period be should be less than 10 Min. High gas pressure should be
avoided.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS > Clause 5.5 Dams used for conserving inert gas during purging shall be removed after completion of the
text:
5.5. Dams used for conserving inert gas during purging shall be removed after completion of the
welding and shall be accounted for. Where ever removal of Dams is not possible, water
soluble Dams should be used.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 5 SHIELDING AND PURGING GAS > Clause 5.6 For socket type of welding purging is not required.
text: 5.6. For socket type of welding purging is not required.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 6 WELDING PROCESS
text: 6 WELDING PROCESS

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 6 WELDING PROCESS > Clause 6.1 Welding of various materials under this specification shall be carried out using one or more
text:
6.1. Welding of various materials under this specification shall be carried out using one or more
following process.
· Shielded Metal Arc Welding Process (SMAW)
· Gas Tungsten Arc Welding Process (GTAW)

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 6 WELDING PROCESS > Clause 6.2 The welding process to be used are given in the welding specification chart. Any deviation
text:
6.2. The welding process to be used are given in the welding specification chart. Any deviation
desired by the Contractor shall be approved by Owner / CONSULTANT.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 6 WELDING PROCESS > Clause 6.3 Automatic and semi-automatic process shall be used only with the approval of Owner /
text:
6.3. Automatic and semi-automatic process shall be used only with the approval of Owner /
CONSULTANT. The welding procedure adopted and consumables used shall be specifically
approved.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 6 WELDING PROCESS > Clause 6.4 Combination of different welding processes could be used for a particular joint only after duly
text:
6.4 Combination of different welding processes could be used for a particular joint only after duly
qualifying the welding procedure to be adopted and obtaining the approval of Owner /
CONSULTANT.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 7 WELDING PROCEDURE QUALIFICATION
text: 7 WELDING PROCEDURE QUALIFICATION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 7 WELDING PROCEDURE QUALIFICATION
text:
Welding procedure qualification shall be carried out in accordance with the relevant
requirements of ASME Sec. IX latest edition or other applicable codes and the job
requirements. The Contractor shall submit the proposed welding procedure specifications
required for the execution of the job in format as per Section IX immediately after the receipt
of the order. Owner/CONSULTANT inspector will review, check and approve the proposed
welding procedure submitted and shall release the procedure for qualification Record (PQR)
tests. The procedure qualification test shall be carried out by the Contractor at his own
expense. A complete set of test results in the format as per Section IX shall be submitted to
the Owner/CONSULTANT inspector for his approval immediately after completing the
procedure qualification test and at least 2 weeks before the commencement of actual work.
Standard test as specified in the code shall be carried out in all cases. In addition to these
tests, other test like macro/micro examination, hardness test, dye penetrant examination,

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 7 WELDING PROCEDURE QUALIFICATION
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 7 WELDING PROCEDURE QUALIFICATION
text:
charpy V-notch, Corrosion tests, impact tests etc. shall be carried out on specimens
depending upon the type of base material, operating conditions and requirements laid down
in the detailed drawings and specifications. It shall be the responsibility of the Contractor to
carry out all the tests required to the satisfaction of the Owner / Consultant inspector. Based
on the acceptance of PQR, WPS shall be prepared for Owner / Contractor approval.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 8 WELDER’S QUALIFICATION
text: 8 WELDER’S QUALIFICATION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 8 WELDER’S QUALIFICATION
text: a. Welders shall be qualified in accordance with the ASME Section-IX or other applicable codes. The Owners inspector shall witness the test and certify the qualification of each welder separately. Only those welders who have been approved by the Owner / Consultant Inspector shall be employed for welding. Contractor shall submit the welder qualification test reports in the format as per Section IX and obtain express approval before commencement of work. It shall be the responsibility of Contractor to carry out qualification tests of welders. For welding of the steam piping, falling under the purview of Indian Boiler Regulations only those welders with IBR Certification, qualified by Boiler Inspectorate, and acceptable to the local Boiler Inspector authority shall be employed. b. The welders shall always have in their possession the identification card as issued by Contractor and shall produce it on demand by the Owner / Consultant inspector. It shall be the responsibility of the Contractor to issue the identity cards after it has been duly certified by the Inspector. c. No welder shall be permitted to work without the possession of identity card. d. If a welder is found to perform a type of welding or in a position for which he is not qualified, he shall be debarred from doing any further work. All welds performed by an unqualified welder shall be cut and redone by a qualified welder at the expense of the Contractor. e. Welder performance

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 8 WELDER’S QUALIFICATION
text: for which he is not qualified, he shall be debarred from doing any further work. All welds performed by an unqualified welder shall be cut and redone by a qualified welder at the expense of the Contractor. e. Welder performance record shall be made for each welder.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 9 EDGE PREPARATION
text: 9 EDGE PREPARATION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 9 EDGE PREPARATION > Clause 9.1 The edges to be welded shall be prepared to meet joint design requirement by any of the
text:
9.1. The edges to be welded shall be prepared to meet joint design requirement by any of the
following method.
a. Carbon Steel – Gas cutting, machining or grinding method shall be used.
b. Low alloy steel (Containing up to 5% chromium) - Gas cutting, machining or grinding
method shall be used.
c. High Alloy Steel (> 5% chromium) and stainless steel, Nickel Alloy – Plasma cutting,

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 9 EDGE PREPARATION
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 9 EDGE PREPARATION
text:
machining, or grinding method shall be used.
d. For tapping magnetic drill shall be used wherever it is possible to use.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 9 EDGE PREPARATION > Clause 9.2 The ends of the weld shall be properly clean to remove paint, oil, grease, rust, oxides, sand,
text:
9.2. The ends of the weld shall be properly clean to remove paint, oil, grease, rust, oxides, sand,
earth and other foreign matters. The end shall be dry before the welding commences.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 9 EDGE PREPARATION > Clause 9.3 Wire brush used for cleaning stainless steel joint shall have stainless steel wires and the
text:
9.3. Wire brush used for cleaning stainless steel joint shall have stainless steel wires and the
grinding wheel used for grinding stainless steel shall be of suitable type. Separate grinding
wheels and wire brushes should be used for carbon steel and stainless steel.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 10 ALINGNMENT AND SPACING
text: 10 ALINGNMENT AND SPACING

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 10 ALINGNMENT AND SPACING > Clause 10.1 Components to be welded shall be aligned and spaced as per requirement laid down in
text:
10.1. Components to be welded shall be aligned and spaced as per requirement laid down in
applicable code. Flame heating for adjustment and correction of ends is not permitted unless
specifically approved by Owner / Consultant.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 10 ALINGNMENT AND SPACING > Clause 10.2 Tack welds for maintaining the alignment of pipe joints shall be made only by qualified welders
text:
10.2. Tack welds for maintaining the alignment of pipe joints shall be made only by qualified welders
using approved WPS. Defective tack welds must be removed prior to the actual welding of
the joints. Tacking cleats to be used in place of direct tacking (Pipe to Fittings / Pipe to Pipe )
and tacking clets shall be of same material as of pipe & fittings.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 10 ALINGNMENT AND SPACING > Clause 10.3 Tack weld should be equally spaced. Minimum number of tacks shall be
text:
10.3. Tack weld should be equally spaced. Minimum number of tacks shall be
a. 3 Tacks – for 2” and smaller dia pipe
b. 4 tacks – for 3 to 12” dia pipes
c. 6 Tacks – for 14” and above larger pipes.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 10 ALINGNMENT AND SPACING > Clause 10.4 Welding shall commence only after approval of fit up by welding inspector.
text: 10.4. Welding shall commence only after approval of fit up by welding inspector.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 11 WEATHER CONDITIONS
text: 11 WEATHER CONDITIONS

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 11 WEATHER CONDITIONS > Clause 11.1 The parts being welded and welding personnel should be adequately protected from rain and
text:
11.1. The parts being welded and welding personnel should be adequately protected from rain and
strong winds. In the absence of such a protection no welding shall be carried out.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 11 WEATHER CONDITIONS > Clause 11.2 During field welding using GTAW process particular care shall be exercised to prevent any
text:
11.2. During field welding using GTAW process particular care shall be exercised to prevent any
air current affecting the process.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP
text: 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP > 12.1 ROOT PASS
text: 12.1 ROOT PASS

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP > 12.1 ROOT PASS
text:
a. Root pass shall be made with electrode / filler wires recommended in the welding
specification chart. Prefer size of electrode is 2.5 mm dia but in any case not less than 1.6
mm or greater than 3.15mm
b. Upward technic shall be adopted for welding pipe held fixed with its axis horizontal.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP > 12.1 ROOT PASS
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP > 12.1 ROOT PASS
text:
c. The root pass of butt joint should be executed so as to achieve full penetration with
complete fusion of the root edges. Weld projection inside the pipe shall be as per
applicable code.
d. Welding shall be uninterrupted
e. Penning shall not be used.
f. Golden / Closure / Tie-inn Joints : Root pass welding shall be with GTAW process only.
Root visual inspection with DPT/MPT is mandatory to perform.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP > 12.2 JOINT COMPLETION
text: 12.2 JOINT COMPLETION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP > 12.2 JOINT COMPLETION
text:
a. Joint shall be completed using the class of electrode recommended in the approved
WPS. Size of electrode shall not exceed 4mm in dia for stainless steel and alloy steel
used for low temperature application.
b. Butt joints shall be completed with a covered layer that would affect good fusion at the
joint edges and a gradual notch free surface.
c. Each weld joints shall have workmanship like finish. Weld identification work shall be
stamped clearly at each joint adjacent to the weld. Metal stamping shall not be used for
this pipe having wall thickness of less than 3.5mm. Suitable paint shall be used on thin
wall pipes for identification.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP > 12.3 DISSIMILAR WELD
text: 12.3 DISSIMILAR WELD

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 12 WELDING TECHNIQUE AND WORKMANSHIP > 12.3 DISSIMILAR WELD
text:
a. Where welds are to be produced between carbon steel and alloy steel, pre heat and
PWHT requirement shall be those specified for corresponding alloy steel material.
b. For welds between two dissimilar Cr.Mo. low alloy steel pre heat and PWHT shall be
those specified for higher alloy steel.
c. For carbon steel / alloy steel to stainless steel welds use of filler wire / electrodes E/ER-
309/E-310/ E NiCr Fe-3 shall be made.
d. The welding procedure, electrode filler wires to be used shall be approved by Owner /
Consultant.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT
text: 13 HEAT TREATMENT

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.1 PREHEATING
text: 13.1 PREHEATING

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.1 PREHEATING
text:
a. No welding shall be performed without pre heating the joints to 10 deg C when the ambient
temperature is below 10 deg C.
b. Preheating requirement of various materials shall be as per welding specification chart.
c. Preheating shall be performed using resistance or induction heating method. Preheating

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.1 PREHEATING
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.1 PREHEATING
text:
by gas burner with neutral flame shall be carried out only if permitted by Owner /
Consultant.
d. Preheating shall be extended not less than 50mm on both sides of the weld.
e. Preheating temperature shall be maintained during welding.
f. Approved temperature, indicating crayons, thermocouples or digital contact
pyrometers, shall be used to measure preheat and interpass temperatures.
g. Preheating for stainless steel is not recommended. Check of interpass temperature for
stainless steel shall be done by digital temperature gauge.
h. The preheat zone shall extend 75 mm or a distance equal to four (4) times the material
thickness, whichever is the greater, beyond each edge of the weld.
i. The maximum preheats and interpass temperature for carbon steel and low-alloy steel
shall be 300°C.
j. The maximum interpass temperature for austenitic stainless steel shall be 175°C

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.2 POST HEATING
text: 13.2 POST HEATING

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.2 POST HEATING
text:
Unless otherwise specified more stringently in other specifications (Example:- Statutory,
Licensor specification etc.) for alloy steel materials such as Cr.Mo. steel, if the PWHT is
not performed immediately after welding, the weld joint and adjacent portion of the pipe
at least 50mm on either side of the weld shall be uniformly heated to 315 deg C and
maintained for half an hour minimum. The joint shall than be wrapped in insulation before
allowing it to cool to room temperature.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)
text: 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)
text:
a. PWHT shall be in accordance with the referenced code and additional requirements
of this specification. A written procedure describing the general PWHT requirements shall
be submitted for review and approval to Owner / Consultant. The PWHT procedure
shall include descriptions of equipment, method of heating, location and type of heating
elements, temperature measurement and thermocouple locations.
b. Prior to the start of PWHT, the Contractor shall prepare a table, listing each joint or
component requiring heat treatment and submit to Owner / Consultant for review. The
table shall include the following information for each joint or component: Location, drawing
number, diameter, wall thickness, material, heating rate, cooling rate, soak temperature
and soak time.
c. Heating and cooling rates shall conform to referenced code or standard or approved

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)
text: WPS. d. PWHT shall follow all welding, including support clips, and structural parts and repairs, but shall be performed prior to any hydro test or other load test. e. PWHT shall be carried out using one (1) or more of the following types of heat sources: · Permanent or semi-permanent furnaces using gas or electric heaters. The furnace atmosphere shall be selected to avoid excessive oxidation during the heat treatment. Direct flame impingement by furnace burners or torch flames is prohibited. · Electric resistance heaters. f. Thermocouples and a calibrated temperature chart au t o recorder shall be used to provide accurate and legible record of all PWHT’s. g. All thermocouple attachments shall be adequately insulated to avoid temperature misreading caused by effect of radiation. h. Temperature r ecor der s shall be calibrated ever y six ( 6) months and a r ecor d shall be maintained on a recorder. The o r i g i n al calibration certificate shall be available for the review by Owner / Consultant. i. Prior to the start of PWHT, components shall be checked to ensure that all restraints are removed and the component is free to expand. j. Surface to the component subject to a heat treatment shall be dry and free from oil, grease, paint etc. k. All machined surfaces, such as flange faces, bolts holes, threads etc., shall be protected from oxidation during the heat treatment. l. All PWHT

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)
text: component subject to a heat treatment shall be dry and free from oil, grease, paint etc. k. All machined surfaces, such as flange faces, bolts holes, threads etc., shall be protected from oxidation during the heat treatment. l. All PWHT chart records shall be correctly identified and shall indicate as minimum: · The weld number and the corresponding thermocouple number with their location. · Heat treatment type. · Temperature and time scales. · Heat treatment date. m. After completion of the PWHT, all thermocouples shall be removed and the attachment areas shall be grounded smooth to clean sound metal and DPT to be carried out. n. Welding or heating after the final PWHT is not permitted without approval of Owner / Consultant. o. The maximum variation in temperature within the heated zone shall not exceed 30°C during the soaking period. p. The temperature attained by the portion under heat treatment shall be recorded by thermocouple. Adequate number of thermocouple should be attached to the pipe directly

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT)
text:
at equally spaced location along the periphery of the pipe joint. The minimum number of
thermocouples attached per joint shall be
- One up to 6” dia pipe
- Two up to 10” dia Pipe
- Three for 12” dia and above.
q. Automatic temperature recorder suitably calibrated should use for measuring and
recording the temperature.
r. Immediately on completion of heat treatment PWHT chart record alomngwith hardness
test result on the weld joints shall be submitted to Owner / Consultant for approval.
s. Each joint shall bear an identification number which shall be maintained in the piping

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.3 POST WELD HEAT TREATMENT (PWHT) > sketch / isometric and on corresponding PWHT chart.
text:
sketch / isometric and on corresponding PWHT chart.
t. Welding Procedure Specification is to be prepared, describing the low energy capacitor
discharge equipment, the combination of materials to be joined, and the technique of
application; qualification of the welding procedure is not required.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.4 WELD HARDNESS TESTING
text: 13.4 WELD HARDNESS TESTING

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.4 WELD HARDNESS TESTING > sketch / isometric and on corresponding PWHT chart.
text:
Weld hardness testing of production welds as required by ASME B31.3 shall be
performed in accordance with ASME B31.3.
i. Testing shall be conducted with portable hardness testers (Microdur or approved
equivalent).
ii. Contractor shall document the results of all hardness tests for review by Owner/
Consultant.
iii. All hardness readings in excess of values shown above shall be reported to Owner /
Consultant. Any corrective actions shall be reviewed with Owner / Consultant,
before proceeding.
iv. Non ferrous materials and austenitic stainless steel welds shall not require
hardness testing, regardless of the service.
v. If PWHT is applied, hardness testing shall be performed after PWHT.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.5 FERRITE MEASUREMENT
text: 13.5 FERRITE MEASUREMENT

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.5 FERRITE MEASUREMENT > sketch / isometric and on corresponding PWHT chart.
text:
a. The ferrite content of first two (2) austenitic stainless steel production welds made by
each welder using the SMAW process, shall be checked in the as welded condition
using a ferrite scope. The ferrite content shall be between 3% and 10%, unless specified
otherwise. Calibration of the magnetic instrument will conform to current AWS – A4.2
practice.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.5 FERRITE MEASUREMENT
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.5 FERRITE MEASUREMENT
text:
b. A minimum of six (6) ferrite readings will be taken on the surface to be tested.
c. Type 310 welds are exempt from this requirement.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.6 ALLOY VERIFICATION
text: 13.6 ALLOY VERIFICATION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.6 ALLOY VERIFICATION
text:
Contractor shall implement a material control system to ensure that materials can be identified
and traced from source to their installed location and associated with the appropriate
certification. 100% colour code implementation for the material shall be ensured from vendor
shop for all piping materials. The methods of controlling the materials to be used and erected
at site are to be established and implemented at site. It includes requirements for receiving,
storing and releasing of all materials and equipment including the field purchased materials
for the construction.
Contractor supplied materials shall be with original, or verified copies of original certification.
Material Certificates shall be made available to Owner/Consultant for review.
Full traceability of piping materials shall be formally documented and maintained throughout
fabrication, painting, erection and test activities. Where piping materials have not been
maintained by traceability, Contractor shall undertake the necessary testing to demonstrate
that all the material used in the pressure loop is acceptable to specification, to the satisfaction
of the Owner / Consultant. Testing may include PMI and/or the chemical analysis and
mechanical testing of samples. Consultant / Owner has right to have any random material
sample tested at third party lab at contractor cost. 100% traceability is required for all lines
other than carbon steel within a specified temperature and pressure range.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.7 CLEANING OF THE WELD JOINT
text: 13.7 CLEANING OF THE WELD JOINT

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.7 CLEANING OF THE WELD JOINT
text:
All weld joint shall be free from adherent, weld spatters, slag, dirt or foreign matters. This can
be achieved by brushing / grinding. For stainless steel, brushes with stainless steel bristle
should be used.
Suitable re-agent shall be applied for SS Loint cleaning.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text: 13.8 INSPECTION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text:
a. Prior to star t of welding, inspection pr ocedures & NDT char t shall be established
in accor dance with reference code. A written procedure for each inspection method and
technique (including acceptance criteria) to be used shall be submitted to Owner /
Consultant. Inspection procedures in conformance with other standards are acceptable
only with approval of Owner / Consultant.
i. Non- destructive examinations (NDE) for final acceptance shall be performed after any

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text: PWHT. Additional NDE may be performed by Contractor prior to PWHT to ensure all reject able indications have been removed. ii. Surface irregular, including cap welds, inhibiting accurate interpretation of the specified method of NDE shall be ground smooth. iii. The minimum extent of examination shall be based on the requirements of ASME B31.3 and approved NDT Chart. iv. Random examination on a percentage basis, for example 5% of pipe welds, is defined below: Examination is defined as 100% inspection of 5% of the production welds for each day production made by each welder. The first two (2) production welds made, by each welder, shall be included in the 5%. All welders, welding processes, pipe sizes and materials shall be represented as a minimum. The examination shall be concurrent with the fabrication. For each weld found to be defective, two (2) additional representative welds shall be examined at contractor’s own cost. In case these additional welds are free from defects, only the defects indicated in the first Examination shall be repaired and re- examined. However, if either of the two (2) additional welds shows defects, all welds represented shall be either: · Fully non-destructively examined and repaired as necessary, or · Completely replaced. v. Any welder or welding operator having a weld rejection rate exceeding 10% of the deposited weld length shall be disqualified from the site or be subject to 100% non- destructive examination until the rejection rate reaches

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text: · Completely replaced. v. Any welder or welding operator having a weld rejection rate exceeding 10% of the deposited weld length shall be disqualified from the site or be subject to 100% non- destructive examination until the rejection rate reaches a level previously agreed upon between Contractors and Owner / Consultant. vi. Non-destructive testing shall be performed by personnel qualified in accordance with ASNT recommended practice SNT-TC -1A, minimum level 2 or approved equivalent. Certification of personnel shall be made available by the Contractor. Contractor's inspectors, responsible for the performance of visual inspections, shall be adequately trained in both welding and inspection. vii. Inspection at the mill, shop or fabrication yard shall not release Contractor from responsibility for repairing or replacing any defective material or workmanship that may be subsequently discovered in the field. b. VISUAL INSPECTION i. All welds shall be visually inspected by Contractor after completion. Each weld shall be uniform in width and size throughout its full length. In addition, the cover passes

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text:
(final weld layer) shall be free of course ripples, grooves, overlaps, arc strikes, abrupt
ridges and valleys. No undercutting is allowed.
c. RADIOGRAPHIC EXAMINATION
i. Performance of the radiography examination of welds shall be in accordance with
the referenced code.
ii. Acceptance criteria shall be conforming the referenced code.
iii. Radiography examination of welds in NPS 2 ½ " pipe or smaller shall be performed by
the elliptical projection technique. At least two (2) separate exposures are required at
locations 90 ˚ apart.
iv. If a pressure-containing weld will not be hydro tested, with prior approval of Owner /
Consultant, it shall be 100% radiography examined in accordance with the referenced
code.
v. In case radiography examination is not practical, then other NDE method shall be specified.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text: d. LIQUID PENETRANT EXAMINATION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text:
i. Performance of the liquid penetrant examination of welds shall be in accordance with
the referenced code.
ii. Acceptance criteria shall conform the referenced code.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text: e. MAGNETIC PARTICLE EXAMINATION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text:
i. Performance of the magnetic particle examination of welds shall be in accordance with
the referenced code.
ii. Acceptance criteria shall be conforming the referenced code.
iii. Permanent magnet yokes are not permitted.
iv. Magnetic particle examination shall be performed on any structural attachments on
pressure-retaining components made of ferromagnetic material that are not hydro tested
after making the attachment weld.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text: f. ULTRASONIC EXAMINATION

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text:
i. Performance of the ultrasonic examination of welds shall be in accordance with the
referenced code.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.8 INSPECTION
text:
ii. Acceptance criteria shall be conforming the referenced code.
iii. Ultrasonic testing may be substituted for radiography if approved by Owner / Consultant.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.9 REPAIRS
text: 13.9 REPAIRS

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.9 REPAIRS
text: a. No welding joints shall be repaired more than twice. If the weld is still not acceptable after the second repair, Owner / Consultant shall have the sole authority to permit additional repair attempt or to require the entire weld be cut out. b. Cracked welds shall be cut out and re-welded. c. Repair welding shall be performed using a properly qualified and approved procedure. In- process repairs (i.e. repairs performed prior to completion of the joint, using the same welding procedure as for the original fabrication) during production does not require a separate procedure. d. All repaired welds shall, as a minimum, be inspected using the original testing method. Additional test methods may also be required, if deemed necessary by O w n er / Consultant. e. Damage to the base metal or welds, including dimensional changes caused by external forces, requires special repair and inspection procedures to be submitted to Owner / Consultant for review and approval prior to undertaking the repairs. f. Any weld not meeting the acceptance criteria of the referenced code or standard shall cut out or repaired. g. Repair of surface defects in welds by chipping, grinding and gouging are permitted provided the minimum required wall thickness plus corrosion allowance is maintained. Removal of defects shall be verified by magnetic particle or liquid penetrant examination. Repair welding of minor defects, as defined below, shall be done by qualified welders using a qualified welding procedure.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.9 REPAIRS
text: required wall thickness plus corrosion allowance is maintained. Removal of defects shall be verified by magnetic particle or liquid penetrant examination. Repair welding of minor defects, as defined below, shall be done by qualified welders using a qualified welding procedure. h. Major defects in welds and base materials, as defined in below table shall be repaired only after a written procedure for each individual repair has been approved by Owner / Consultant. The written procedure shall include a sketch detailing extent and location of the defect, how defects will be removed, how removal will be verified, qualified welding procedure to be used, subsequent heat treatment and non-destructive examination.

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.10 DOCUMENTS TO BE SUBMITTED BY CONTRACTOR (4 COPIES EACH)
text: 13.10 DOCUMENTS TO BE SUBMITTED BY CONTRACTOR (4 COPIES EACH)

breadcrumb: 02. Construction Quality Requirement Plant > 13 HEAT TREATMENT > 13.10 DOCUMENTS TO BE SUBMITTED BY CONTRACTOR (4 COPIES EACH)
text:
a. Batch test Certificates, for the electrodes used, obtained from the Electrode
Manufacturers.
b. Proposed Heat Treatment Procedure.
c. Heat Treatment Charts.
d. Weld joint hardness test results.
e. Welding Procedure Specifications as per ASME Section-IX immediately after receipt of
the order.
f. Welding Procedure Qualification records as per ASME Section-IX.
g. Welder Performance Qualification records as per ASME Section-IX immediately after
conducting Welder Qualification Test.
h. Radiography Procedure and other NDT procedures.
i. Radiography test Report along with Radiography and other NDT reports.
Followings are the welding specification for guidelines / reference purpose only. Specific requirement
like welding specification, preheating, post heating, PWHT, NDT etc. as per applicable codes and
specification requirement shall be followed.
